channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Man convicted of manslaughter in Surrey bus stabbing sentenced to eight years
item.description: In Surrey, British Columbia, the sentencing of Kaden Mintenko, 23, for the fatal stabbing of a 17-year-old boy aboard a transit bus in 2023 sparked strong emotions among the victim’s family and friend...

item.title: Canadians Continue Domestic Travel Despite Rising Costs, Survey Shows
item.description: A recent survey conducted for the Business Development Bank of Canada's annual tourism outlook indicates that Canadians plan to maintain their travel plans in 2026, even in the face of higher transpor...

item.title: Karachi police obtain 3-day remand of woman accused of narcotics trafficking
item.description: In Karachi, the local police have been granted a three-day physical remand of a woman reportedly involved in the supply of cocaine and other dangerous narcotics. Anmol, also known as Pinky, was appreh...

item.title: Defence Minister Warns Taliban Over Cross-Border Terrorism Support
item.description: On Tuesday, Defence Minister Khawaja Asif strongly criticised the Taliban-led government of Afghanistan for allegedly supporting militants and failing to provide written guarantees to stop cross-borde...

item.title: The Last Drink of Outlaw John Shaw After His Death
item.description: In the early morning of April 8, 1905, John Shaw and his partner William Evans, also known as Bill Smythe, robbed the Wigwam Saloon in Winslow, Arizona, taking at least $200 in silver coins. The two y...

item.title: Nedra Talley, Last Original Ronettes Member, Passes Away at 80
item.description: Nedra Talley-Ross (1946–2026), the final surviving original member of the iconic 1960s girl group The Ronettes, passed away on April 26, 2026, at age 80. Her daughter confirmed that Talley-Ross died p...

item.title: N Rangasamy Sworn in as Puducherry Chief Minister for Fifth Term
item.description: On Wednesday, Mr N Rangasamy was sworn in as the Chief Minister of Puducherry for the fifth time, continuing his long-standing political career in the Union Territory. The oath of office was administe...

item.title: Yanga fined Sh30 million for breaching dressing room and match rules
item.description: Young Africans (Yanga), defending champions of the Tanzania Premier League, have been slapped with one of the heaviest fines in the history of Tanzanian football following their Kariakoo Derby match a...

item.title: Nigeria Launches $188 Million Fund to Boost 191MW Solar Energy Access
item.description: The Rural Electrification Agency (REA) join hand with UK PACT, FCMB, and ARM Harith Infrastructure Investment Limited (ARMHIIL) don launch Green Finance Investment Facility (GFiF) to mobilise private ...

item.title: Rigathi Gachagua appears in court as impeachment proceedings continue
item.description: Today, former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua arrived at Milimani High Court for the continuation of his impeachment case. The court proceedings are drawing significant public attention, with many K...

item.title: Atlantic Indigenous Leaders Advocate for Partnership in Offshore Wind Projects
item.description: Indigenous leaders in Atlantic Canada are urging for a meaningful role in the development of offshore wind farms in their regions. Chiefs from several First Nations emphasize the importance of consult...

item.title: Minister Urges Zanzibar Youth to Improve Skills and Take Available Jobs
item.description: Zanzibar — Minister for Labour and Investment, Mr Sharif Ali Sharif, has called on the youth of Zanzibar to stop being picky when it comes to jobs and instead focus on improving their skills. Speaking...

item.title: Kenya Faces Early Setback at Africa Championships as Rwanda Wins Women's 5000m
item.description: Kenya began their campaign at the Africa Championships in Accra on a slightly disappointing note after failing to secure a medal in the women's 5000m. Maurine Chebor, the cross country ace, led for mo...

item.title: Senate Clash Between Padilla and Pangilinan Sparks Calls for Responsible Voting
item.description: This week in the Philippine Senate saw heightened tensions following major political developments. A day after Senator Tito Sotto was ousted as Senate president and an ICC arrest warrant for Senator B...

item.title: Experiencing the Awe of a Total Solar Eclipse
item.description: In August 2017, an Italian-born astronomer travelled from London to Tennessee to witness her first total solar eclipse, having previously seen only partial eclipses. Despite years of studying celestia...

item.title: CBI investigates fraudulent salary payments at Delhi night shelters
item.description: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has initiated an inquiry into alleged financial irregularities in night shelters managed by the Delhi Urban Shelter Improvement Board (DUSIB) in 2023. Prelimi...

item.title: President Ruto Pushes for Sovereign Equality and Mutual Investment in Africa-France Ties at Nairobi Summit
item.description: President William Ruto has strongly called for a fresh win-win partnership between Africa and France based on mutual respect, sovereign equality and shared responsibility. While opening the Africa For...

item.title: Joe Nina pays tribute to Maria McCloy after her passing
item.description: Renowned 90s musician Joe Nina has expressed deep sorrow following the passing of Maria McCloy, a prominent figure in South Africa's arts and entertainment scene. The pair had recently reunited at McC...

item.title: Hanoi Sidewalk Air-Raid Shelters During the 1967 Vietnam War
item.description: This photograph, taken in 1967 by American photojournalist Lee Lockwood for Life magazine, captures the daily life of Hanoi residents during the Vietnam War. The image shows individuals sheltering in ...

item.title: Planned Release of White-tailed Eagles in Exmoor to Proceed Amidst Farmer Concerns
item.description: The UK government has approved the release of up to 20 white-tailed eagles, also known as sea eagles, across Exmoor National Park over the next three years. These birds, which were once extinct in the...
